stem: An employee receives an email that looks as if it is from their bank, asking them to click a link and confirm their password.
model_answer: This is a phishing attack: the attacker sends a fake email (or text) pretending to be from a trusted organisation so the victim will click a link to a counterfeit site and enter their login details. The credentials are then captured by the attacker and can be used to steal money or further data. Phishing is a form of social engineering because it manipulates the person, not the technology.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Explain how this phishing attack works.

mark_points.text: the attacker sends an email/message that pretends to be from a trusted organisation (e.g. the bank)
accepted_alternatives: fake email impersonates a real company

mark_points.text: the link leads to a fake website that looks genuine
accepted_alternatives: user is tricked into visiting a counterfeit site

mark_points.text: the victim enters credentials/personal data which the attacker captures
